steps.title: Translate years into t-values
steps.content: The variable $t$ means years after 2000. So year 2000 is $t=0$ with population $862$, and year 2010 is $t=10$ with population $846$.

steps.title: Fit the model in Desmos
steps.content: Enter the points $(0,862)$ and $(10,846)$ and run $y_1 \sim m x_1+b$. Desmos gives $m=-1.6$ and $b=862$, so the model is $y=862-1.6x$. In the problem's variables, that is $P(t)=862-1.6t$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A matches $P(t)=862-1.6t$. Choice D uses $(t-2000)$ even though $t$ already means years after 2000, so it uses the wrong input variable.
